revision.text:
'''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[浮羽郡]][[吉井町 (福岡県)|吉井町]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
同名の温泉が存在する。[[吉井温泉 (群馬県)|吉井温泉]]を参照せよ。
== アクセス == *鉄道：[[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]よりタクシーで約7分。
== 泉質 == *炭酸水酸化鉄泉 *単純温泉 源泉温度36～42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]沿いに温泉街が広がる。筑後川の対岸は[[原鶴温泉]]である。
旅館などは3軒存在し、こじんまりとした温泉街となっている。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956年]]である。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43年]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== 関連項目 == *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[日本の温泉地一覧]]
== 外部リンク ==
[[Category:温泉|よしいおんせん]]

'''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
[[群馬県]]にある同名の温泉については、[[吉井温泉 (群馬県)]]を参照せよ。
== アクセス == *鉄道：[[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]よりタクシーで約7分。
== 泉質 == *炭酸水酸化鉄泉 *単純温泉 源泉温度36～42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]沿いに温泉街が広がる。筑後川の対岸は[[原鶴温泉]]である。
旅館などは3軒存在し、こじんまりとした温泉街となっている。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956年]]である。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43年]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== 関連項目 == *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[日本の温泉地一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]
[[Category:温泉|よしいおんせん]]

{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |画像 = |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]沿いに温泉街が広がる。筑後川の対岸は[[原鶴温泉]]である。
旅館などは3軒存在し、こじんまりとした温泉街となっている。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956年]]である。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43年]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Unreferenced section|date=November 2018}} '''Open Spring Legends''' (開湯伝説, Kaitō Densetsu) are traditional tales and legends about the origins of the discovery of {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉}}. These are oral traditions and differ from historical fact (explained below). == Overview == The content of these legends varies by onsen, with some having multiple legends. Not all onsen have associated legends, but they are more common among historically older hot springs...."
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced section|date=November 2018}} '''Open Spring Legends''' (開湯伝説, Kaitō Densetsu) are traditional tales and legends about the origins of the discovery of {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉}}. These are oral traditions and differ from historical fact (explained below).
== Overview == The content of these legends varies by onsen, with some having multiple legends. Not all onsen have associated legends, but they are more common among historically older hot springs. The origins often involve mythology, discoveries by monks or samurai, or animals uncovering the springs. These legends were created primarily to "lend credibility" to verbal explanations. For instance, simply telling travelers about a hot spring might not persuade them, but claiming that a mythical bird or an esteemed historical figure discovered it could add convincing narrative weight. In reality, many springs were likely discovered by local farmers or woodsmen.
The primary purpose of these legends was to promote the onsen as "our local spring." Even today, many hot spring areas actively propagate these legends as a form of advertisement, a practice that dates back to the Edo period or earlier.
In modern times, with the advent of technological development like {{ill|borehole drilling|ja|ボーリング}}, hot springs rarely have associated legends. However, in some cases, developers may attribute the discovery to dreams or divine messages, such as the legend of {{ill|Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)|ja|吉井温泉_(福岡県)}}.
== Mythological and Imperial Origins == Many legends feature the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} as discoverers of hot springs. These figures are common in legends surrounding ancient springs from pre-Buddhist times, reflecting reverence for ancestors, heroes, and pioneers. Historically, these mythological figures may have been influential local leaders or practitioners of medicine.
For example: * '''{{ill|Dōgo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}''': The legend claims Sukunahikona danced in the healing waters, leaving his footprints behind on a stone (Tamaishi). Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|ja|鶴見岳}} in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊予国風土記}}. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉}}''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|ja|奥津温泉}}''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|ja|嬉野温泉}}''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|ja|武雄温泉}}''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|ja|南紀白浜温泉}}''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|ja|一遍}}. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|ja|高野聖}} seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|ja|大友頼泰}} established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|ja|浜脇温泉}} and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|ja|別府温泉}}. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉}} through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|ja|火男火売神社}}.
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|ja|下呂温泉}}, {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|ja|山中温泉}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}} * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}} * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉}} * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉}} * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}, {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|ja|鹿教湯温泉}} * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|ja|俵山温泉}} * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|ja|老神温泉}}
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.

{{Unreferenced section|date=November 2018}} '''Open Spring Legends''' (開湯伝説, Kaitō Densetsu) are traditional tales and legends about the origins of the discovery of {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉}}. These are oral traditions and differ from historical fact (explained below).
== Overview == end of {{ill|Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)|ja|吉井温泉_(福岡県)}}.
== Mythological and Imperial Origins == Many legends feature the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} as discoverers of hot springs. These figures are common in legends surrounding ancient springs from pre-Buddhist times, reflecting reverence for ancestors, heroes, and pioneers. Historically, these mythological figures may have been influential local leaders or practitioners of medicine.
For example: * '''{{ill|Dōgo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}''': The legend claims Sukunahikona danced in the healing waters, leaving his footprints behind on a stone (Tamaishi). Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|ja|鶴見岳}} in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊予国風土記}}. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉}}''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|ja|奥津温泉}}''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|ja|嬉野温泉}}''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|ja|武雄温泉}}''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|ja|南紀白浜温泉}}''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|ja|一遍}}. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|ja|高野聖}} seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|ja|大友頼泰}} established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|ja|浜脇温泉}} and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|ja|別府温泉}}. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉}} through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|ja|火男火売神社}}.
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|ja|下呂温泉}}, {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|ja|山中温泉}}, {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}} * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}} * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|ja|湯田温泉}} * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉}} * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}, {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|ja|鹿教湯温泉}} * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|ja|俵山温泉}} * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|ja|老神温泉}}
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.

{{Unreferenced section|date=November 2018]] '''Open Spring Legends''' (開湯伝説, Kaitō Densetsu) are traditional tales and legends about the origins of the discovery of {{ill|onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[zh:温泉]]. These are oral traditions and differ from historical fact (explained below).
== Overview == end of {{ill|Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:吉井温泉_(福岡県)]].
== Mythological and Imperial Origins == Many legends feature the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大国主命]] and {{ill|Sukunahikona|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:少彦名命]] as discoverers of hot springs. These figures are common in legends surrounding ancient springs from pre-Buddhist times, reflecting reverence for ancestors, heroes, and pioneers. Historically, these mythological figures may have been influential local leaders or practitioners of medicine.
For example: * '''{{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:道後温泉]]''': The legend claims Sukunahikona danced in the healing waters, leaving his footprints behind on a stone (Tamaishi). Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鶴見岳]] in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊予国風土記]]. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:有馬温泉]]''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥津温泉]]''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:嬉野温泉]]''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:武雄温泉]]''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:南紀白浜温泉]]''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日本書紀]].
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:行基]], {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:空海]] (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:一遍]]. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仏教]]'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:坂上田村麻呂]], are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高野聖]] seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大友頼泰]] established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:浜脇温泉]] and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:別府温泉]]. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鉄輪温泉]] through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:火男火売神社]].
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:下呂温泉]], {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山中温泉]], {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:道後温泉]] * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:城崎温泉]] * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:湯田温泉]] * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:三朝温泉]] * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山鹿温泉]], {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:俵山温泉]] * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:老神温泉]]
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Onsen Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[zh:温泉神社]] * {{ill|Onsen Temple|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[zh:温泉寺]]

Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鶴見岳]] in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:伊予国風土記]]. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:有馬温泉]]''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:奥津温泉]]''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]]''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:武雄温泉]]''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]]''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:日本書紀]].
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:行基]], {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:空海]] (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:一遍]]. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:仏教]]'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]], are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:高野聖]] seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:別府温泉]]. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:火男火売神社]].
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:下呂温泉]], {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山中温泉]], {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:道後温泉]] * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]], {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:老神温泉]]
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.

Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鶴見岳]] in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:伊予国風土記]]. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:有馬温泉]]''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:奥津温泉]]''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]]''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:武雄温泉]]''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]]''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:日本書紀]].
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:行基]], {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:空海]] (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:一遍]]. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:仏教]]'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]], are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:高野聖]] seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:別府温泉]]. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:火男火売神社]].
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:下呂温泉]], {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山中温泉]], {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:道後温泉]] * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]], {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:老神温泉]]
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.

Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鶴見岳]] in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:伊予国風土記]]. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:有馬温泉]]''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:奥津温泉]]''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]]''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:武雄温泉]]''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]]''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:日本書紀]].
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:行基]], {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:空海]] (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:一遍]]. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:仏教]]'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]], are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:高野聖]] seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:別府温泉]]. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:火男火売神社]].
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:下呂温泉]], {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山中温泉]], {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:道後温泉]] * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]], {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:老神温泉]]
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.

== Mythological and Imperial Origins == Many legends feature the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:大国主命]] and {{ill|Sukunahikona|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:少彦名命]] as discoverers of hot springs. These figures are common in legends surrounding ancient springs from pre-Buddhist times, reflecting reverence for ancestors, heroes, and pioneers. Historically, these mythological figures may have been influential local leaders or practitioners of medicine.
For example: * '''{{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:道後温泉]]''': The legend claims Sukunahikona danced in the healing waters, leaving his footprints behind on a stone (Tamaishi). Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鶴見岳]] in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:伊予国風土記]]. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:有馬温泉]]''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:奥津温泉]]''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]]''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:武雄温泉]]''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]]''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:日本書紀]].
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:行基]], {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:空海]] (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:一遍]]. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:仏教]]'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]], are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:高野聖]] seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:別府温泉]]. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:火男火売神社]].
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:下呂温泉]], {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山中温泉]], {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:道後温泉]] * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]], {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:老神温泉]]
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced section|date=November 2018]] '''Open Spring Legends''' (開湯伝説, Kaitō Densetsu) are traditional tales and legends about the origins of the discovery of {{ill|onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[zh:温泉]]. These are oral traditions and differ from historical fact (explained below).
== Overview == end of {{ill|Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:吉井温泉_(福岡県)]].
== Mythological and Imperial Origins == Many legends feature the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:大国主命]] and {{ill|Sukunahikona|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:少彦名命]] as discoverers of hot springs. These figures are common in legends surrounding ancient springs from pre-Buddhist times, reflecting reverence for ancestors, heroes, and pioneers. Historically, these mythological figures may have been influential local leaders or practitioners of medicine.
For example: * '''{{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:道後温泉]]''': The legend claims Sukunahikona danced in the healing waters, leaving his footprints behind on a stone (Tamaishi). Ōkuninushi is also said to have drawn hot water from {{ill|Tsurumi-dake|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鶴見岳]] in Ōita to Dōgo through an undersea conduit, as recorded in the lost text of the {{ill|Iyo-no-Kuni Fudoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:伊予国風土記]]. * '''{{ill|Arima Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:有馬温泉]]''': The deities Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona discovered the spring after observing three crows bathing to heal their wounds. * '''{{ill|Okutsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:奥津温泉]]''': Legends of imperial visits persist throughout western Japan. * '''{{ill|Ureshino Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]]''' and '''{{ill|Takeo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:武雄温泉]]''': Linked to Empress Jingū. * '''{{ill|Nanki Shirahama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]]''': Associated with Empress Jitō and other figures mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:日本書紀]].
== Legends of Discovery by Historical Figures == Discoveries attributed to historical figures often involve monks such as {{ill|Gyōki|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:行基]], {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:空海]] (Kōbō Daishi), or {{ill|Ippen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:一遍]]. These stories reflect {{ill|Buddhism|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:仏教]]'s emphasis on bathing as a way to dispel sickness and bring blessings, with monks often facilitating baths for the poor, ill, or prisoners.
Legends of discovery by military commanders, such as {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]], are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. Many such attributions borrow names of famous figures for credibility. For instance, stories linking onsen discoveries to Kūkai often reflect the efforts of {{ill|Kōya hijiri|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:高野聖]] seeking new springs and associating them with their founder.
Legends involving prominent figures became more historically grounded during the Kamakura period, reflecting improved medical knowledge and the practical benefits of hot springs. For example: * '''Kamakura Period:''' {{ill|Ōtomo Yoriyasu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] established hot spring magistrates to develop {{ill|Hamawaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] and {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:別府温泉]]. * '''Ippen:''' Stopped in Beppu in 1276, discovering {{ill|Tetsu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] through a divine message from {{ill|Hi no Kami|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:火男火売神社]].
== Legends of Discovery by Animals == Some legends credit animals with discovering onsen, often featuring "white" animals symbolizing purity or divine guidance. These include: * '''Herons:''' {{ill|Gero Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:下呂温泉]], {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山中温泉]], {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:道後温泉]] * '''Cranes:''' {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] * '''White Foxes:''' {{ill|Yuda Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] * '''White Wolves:''' {{ill|Misasa Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] * '''Deer:''' {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]], {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] * '''Monkeys:''' {{ill|Tawarayama Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] * '''Snakes:''' {{ill|Oigami Onsen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[ja:老神温泉]]
In reality, villagers, hunters, or woodsmen likely discovered the springs after observing animals bathing in them. The association with animals was likely used to make the story more relatable and convincing.
== References == * Noguchi, Fuyuhito. *Comprehensive Encyclopedia of Hot Springs Nationwide* (Travel Yomiuri Publishing, 1997), pp. 706–710.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Onsen Shrine|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[zh:温泉神社]] * {{ill|Onsen Temple|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-qq]] [[zh:温泉寺]]
{{Onsen-stub]]

off this bad category]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |画像 = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956年]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43年]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

{{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}}

== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956年]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43年]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。

== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956年]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43年]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]
{{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}}

Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

(Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]
{{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:よしいおんせん}} [[Category:福岡県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:うきは市の地理]]

[[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。

[[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。

[[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

'''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

= |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。

[[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]]{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。

[[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]]{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

[[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]]{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] [[Category:Qq]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]]{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。

対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。

開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。

== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] [[Category:Qq]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]]{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 吉井温泉 |image = Yoshii onsen.JPG |コメント = |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]]、炭酸水酸化鉄泉 |泉温 = 36 - 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }} '''吉井温泉'''（よしいおんせん）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]（旧国[[筑後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * 炭酸水酸化鉄泉 * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]36〜42℃
== 温泉街 == [[筑後川]]の左岸に位置しており、旅館や日帰り温泉施設が存在する<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url =https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title = 吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website =環境省 |accessdate = 2024-11-29}}</ref>。筑後川の対岸には[[原鶴温泉]]がある。
対岸原鶴温泉同様、[[鵜飼い]]ならびに[[アユ|鮎]]料理が温泉街の名物となっている。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1956]]である<ref name="env" />。神のお告げにより温泉を発見したとされ、「神の瀬温泉」の異名も持つ。
開湯から12年後の[[1968年|昭和43]][[11月19日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第451号により、[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 : [[久大本線]][[筑後吉井駅]]より[[タクシー]]で約7分。
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm 久留米広域市町村圏事務組合 観光情報【温泉・宿泊】吉井温泉]
{{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:よしいおんせん}} [[Category:福岡県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:うきは市の地理]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yoshii Onsen (Fukuoka)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yoshii Onsen (吉井温泉) | image = Yoshii onsen.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} | Access = Rail: From the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}} at {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}}, take a {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} for about 7 minutes. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} | Spring Temperature = 36 – 42°C | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm Kurume Broad Area Municipal Association Tourism Information [Hot Springs & Lodging] – Yoshii Onsen]

{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yoshii Onsen (吉井温泉) | image = Yoshii onsen.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} | Access = Rail: From the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}} at {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}}, take a {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} for about 7 minutes. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} | Spring Temperature = 36 – 42°C | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kttnet.co.jp/kurume-koiki/html/kanko/html/kanko0602.htm Kurume Broad Area Municipal Association Tourism Information [Hot Springs & Lodging] – Yoshii Onsen]

{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yoshii Onsen (吉井温泉) | image = Yoshii onsen.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} | Access = Rail: From the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}} at {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}}, take a {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} for about 7 minutes. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} | Spring Temperature = 36 – 42°C | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.

{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yoshii Onsen (吉井温泉) | image = Yoshii onsen.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} | Access = Rail: From the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}} at {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}}, take a {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} for about 7 minutes. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} | Spring Temperature = 36 – 42°C | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.

'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.

{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yoshii Onsen (吉井温泉) | image = Yoshii onsen.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} | Access = Rail: From the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}} at {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}}, take a {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} for about 7 minutes. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} | Spring Temperature = 36 – 42°C | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.

{{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yoshii Onsen (吉井温泉) | image = Yoshii onsen.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} | Access = Rail: From the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}} at {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}}, take a {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} for about 7 minutes. | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} | Spring Temperature = 36 – 42°C | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }}
'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.

'''Yoshii Onsen''' (吉井温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Ukiha City|ja|うきは市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国}}).
== Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate and Ferric Hydroxide Hot Spring|ja|炭酸水酸化鉄泉}} * {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} ** {{ill|Source Temperature|ja|源泉温度}} 36–42°C
== Hot Spring Town == The hot spring town is located on the left bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}}, where inns and day-use hot spring facilities can be found.<ref name="env">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/area/pdf/hoyo_056.pdf|title=吉井温泉 国民保養温泉地計画書|website={{ill|Ministry of the Environment|ja|環境省}}|accessdate=2024-11-29}}</ref> On the opposite bank of the Chikugo River lies {{ill|Hara-Tsuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉}}.
Like Hara-Tsuru Onsen, the hot spring town is also renowned for its specialties in {{ill|cormorant fishing|ja|鵜飼い}} and {{ill|ayu|ja|鮎}} cuisine.
== History == The hot spring was first opened in {{ill|1956|ja|1956}}. It is said to have been discovered through a divine revelation, earning it the alternative name {{ill|Kami no Se Onsen|ja|神の瀬温泉}}.
Twelve years later, on November 19, 1968 (Showa 43), by notification No. 451 issued by the {{ill|Ministry of Health and Welfare|ja|厚生労働省|lt=Ministry of Health and Welfare}}, it was designated as a {{ill|National Public Resort Hot Spring Area|ja|国民保養温泉地}}.
== Access == * Rail: About 7 minutes by {{ill|taxi|ja|タクシー}} from {{ill|Chikugo Yoshii Station|ja|筑後吉井駅}} on the {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|ja|久大本線}}.
